description: Prompt asking the model to diagnose, debug, or fix a piece of code. Must include the code, the symptom, and ideally what the user has already tried.

checks.description: Code is fenced (triple-backtick), inline-coded, or indented as a block.
Language is named so the model can apply the right idioms and tooling.

checks.pattern: \b(expect|expected|should|supposed to|meant to|ought to|i want|i need it to|the goal is)\b
checks.description: What the code is supposed to do is stated.
What the code is actually doing is stated.
An actual error message, exception name, or stack trace is included.

checks.pattern: \b(i tried|i've tried|already tried|attempted|i checked|i verified|i confirmed|i ran|i tested)\b
checks.description: User has stated what they've already tried so the model doesn't suggest the same.
Snippet is under 3000 chars — debug prompts longer than that are usually un-minimized and waste tokens.
An explicit question or imperative — not just 'here's broken code'.
